100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 7, 1926, tensions escalated in the Far East as Japan prepared to strengthen its military presence in China, raising concerns among Western powers. In Europe, the League of Nations faced criticism over its handling of disarmament talks, with member states divided on the issue. Meanwhile, in the United States, the ongoing debate over Prohibition continued to stir public sentiment, as calls for repeal gained momentum.
Updated: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 07:47:09 AM CDT
